message:
✨ Extend missions with uninstall, upgrade, and troubleshooting sections (#245)

* ✨ Extend mission schema with uninstall, upgrade, and troubleshooting sections

- Update LLM prompt to request uninstall, upgrade, and troubleshooting steps
- Add new sections to buildMissionJson output (uninstall, upgrade, troubleshooting arrays)
- Add quality score bonuses for complete sections (+5 each for uninstall/upgrade/troubleshooting)
- Uninstall: 2-4 steps for clean removal (release, CRDs, namespace)
- Upgrade: 2-4 steps for update workflow (backup, update, verify, rollback)
- Troubleshooting: 3-5 common issues with diagnostic commands and fixes

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* ✨ Add section completeness check to workflow

- New 'Section completeness' review step checks for install/uninstall/upgrade/troubleshooting
- Reports percentage of missions with all 4 sections
- Added as soft gate (warning, not blocking) in verdict table
- Added to step summary output

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -356,6 +356,46 @@ jobs:
             echo "detail=All $TOTAL missions have CLI commands" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
           fi
 
+      # ── Section Completeness Check ─────────────────────────────
+      - name: 'Review: Section completeness'
+        id: completeness
+        if: steps.check.outputs.count != '0'
+        run: |
+          echo "" >> install-report.md
+          echo "## Section Completeness" >> install-report.md
+          TOTAL=0
+          COMPLETE=0
+          for f in $(find solutions/cncf-install -name '*.json' ! -name '.gitkeep' ! -name '*.draft.json'); do
+            TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
+            SECTIONS=$(node -e "
+              const d=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('$f','utf-8'));
+              const m = d.mission || {};
+              const has = [];
+              if ((m.steps || []).length > 0) has.push('install');
+              if ((m.uninstall || []).length > 0) has.push('uninstall');
+              if ((m.upgrade || []).length > 0) has.push('upgrade');
+              if ((m.troubleshooting || []).length > 0) has.push('troubleshooting');
+              console.log(has.join(',') + '|' + has.length + '/4');
+            ")
+            SECTIONS_LIST=$(echo "$SECTIONS" | cut -d'|' -f1)
+            SECTIONS_COUNT=$(echo "$SECTIONS" | cut -d'|' -f2)
+            if [ "$SECTIONS_COUNT" = "4/4" ]; then
+              echo "  ✅ $(basename $f): $SECTIONS_COUNT ($SECTIONS_LIST)" | tee -a install-report.md
+              COMPLETE=$((COMPLETE + 1))
+            else
+              echo "  ⚠️ $(basename $f): $SECTIONS_COUNT ($SECTIONS_LIST)" | tee -a install-report.md
+            fi
+          done
+          if [ "$TOTAL" -gt 0 ]; then
+            PCT=$((COMPLETE * 100 / TOTAL))
+            echo "result=pass" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "detail=$COMPLETE/$TOTAL complete ($PCT%)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "📊 Section completeness: $COMPLETE/$TOTAL missions have all 4 sections ($PCT%)" | tee -a install-report.md
+          else
+            echo "result=pass" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+            echo "detail=No missions to check" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+          fi
+
       # ── Automated Review Decision ──────────────────────────────
       - name: 'Review: Render verdict'
         id: verdict
@@ -388,12 +428,13 @@ jobs:
           done
 
           # Soft gates — warnings don't block merge
-          for CHECK in links freshness smoketest; do
+          for CHECK in links freshness smoketest completeness; do
             RESULT="${{ steps.links.outputs.result }}"
             DETAIL="${{ steps.links.outputs.detail }}"
             case "$CHECK" in
               freshness) RESULT="${{ steps.freshness.outputs.result }}"; DETAIL="${{ steps.freshness.outputs.detail }}" ;;
               smoketest) RESULT="${{ steps.smoketest.outputs.result }}"; DETAIL="${{ steps.smoketest.outputs.detail }}" ;;
+              completeness) RESULT="${{ steps.completeness.outputs.result }}"; DETAIL="${{ steps.completeness.outputs.detail }}" ;;
             esac
             ICON="✅"
             if [ "$RESULT" = "warn" ]; then
@@ -451,6 +492,7 @@ jobs:
           echo "| Freshness | ${{ steps.freshness.outputs.result }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
           echo "| Index | ${{ steps.index.outputs.result }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
           echo "| Smoke Test | ${{ steps.smoketest.outputs.result }} |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
+          echo "| Completeness | ${{ steps.completeness.outputs.result }} (${{ steps.completeness.outputs.detail }}) |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
           echo "| **Verdict** | **${{ steps.verdict.outputs.verdict }}** |" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
 
       # Upload missions + report for downstream jobs

@@ -193,7 +193,7 @@ async function findDockerfile(owner, repo) {
 // ─── LLM synthesis ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
 
 const INSTALL_SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are an expert Kubernetes technical writer creating INSTALLATION missions for the KubeStellar Console.
-An "install mission" is a structured, copy-pasteable guide that takes a user from zero to a running instance of a CNCF project.
+An "install mission" is a structured, copy-pasteable guide that takes a user from zero to a running instance of a CNCF project, and also covers uninstalling, upgrading, and troubleshooting.
 
 Your output MUST be a JSON object with these fields:
 {
@@ -204,6 +204,24 @@ Your output MUST be a JSON object with these fields:
       "description": "Detailed step with exact commands. Must be copy-pasteable. Use code blocks."
     }
   ],
+  "uninstall": [
+    {
+      "title": "Short imperative title (e.g. 'Remove the Helm release')",
+      "description": "Detailed step to cleanly remove the project. Include cleanup of CRDs, namespaces, PVCs, etc."
+    }
+  ],
+  "upgrade": [
+    {
+      "title": "Short imperative title (e.g. 'Update the Helm repository')",
+      "description": "Detailed step to upgrade/update an existing installation. Include backup steps, version checks, and rollback instructions."
+    }
+  ],
+  "troubleshooting": [
+    {
+      "title": "Short title describing the issue (e.g. 'Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff')",
+      "description": "Description of the problem, how to diagnose it, and the fix. Include exact diagnostic commands."
+    }
+  ],
   "resolution": "2-3 sentences confirming what a successful installation looks like.",
   "difficulty": "beginner|intermediate|advanced",
   "installMethods": ["helm", "kubectl", "operator", "kustomize", "docker"],
@@ -224,7 +242,10 @@ Rules:
 - Include at least 1 post-install configuration step (resource limits, RBAC, TLS, or monitoring)
 - Pin versions — never use :latest
 - Use --namespace and --create-namespace
-- 4-6 steps is ideal
+- 4-6 install steps is ideal
+- "uninstall" must have 2-4 steps covering: remove release, clean up CRDs/resources, remove namespace
+- "upgrade" must have 2-4 steps covering: backup current state, update repo/manifests, upgrade, verify
+- "troubleshooting" must have 3-5 common issues with diagnostic commands and fixes
 - Do NOT invent URLs or image names — only use what's in the provided context`
 
 function buildInstallPrompt(project, context) {
@@ -389,6 +410,13 @@ function applyQualityGate(mission, config) {
   if (/resource.*limit|rbac|tls|certificate/i.test(allText)) adjustedScore += 5
   if (/see docs|see the documentation/i.test(allText) && !INSTALL_CMD_RE.test(allText)) adjustedScore -= 10
   if (/:latest\b/.test(allText)) adjustedScore -= 5
+  // Bonuses for completeness: uninstall, upgrade, troubleshooting sections
+  const uninstallSteps = mission.mission?.uninstall || []
+  const upgradeSteps = mission.mission?.upgrade || []
+  const troubleSteps = mission.mission?.troubleshooting || []
+  if (uninstallSteps.length >= 2) adjustedScore += 5
+  if (upgradeSteps.length >= 2) adjustedScore += 5
+  if (troubleSteps.length >= 3) adjustedScore += 5
   adjustedScore = Math.min(100, Math.max(0, adjustedScore))
 
   let tier
@@ -434,6 +462,18 @@ function buildMissionJson(project, llmResult, context, config) {
         title: s.title.slice(0, 120),
         description: s.description.slice(0, 3000),
       })),
+      uninstall: (llmResult.uninstall || []).map(s => ({
+        title: s.title.slice(0, 120),
+        description: s.description.slice(0, 3000),
+      })),
+      upgrade: (llmResult.upgrade || []).map(s => ({
+        title: s.title.slice(0, 120),
+        description: s.description.slice(0, 3000),
+      })),
+      troubleshooting: (llmResult.troubleshooting || []).map(s => ({
+        title: s.title.slice(0, 120),
+        description: s.description.slice(0, 3000),
+      })),
       resolution: {
         summary: llmResult.resolution || `${displayName} is installed and verified.`,
         codeSnippets: extractCodeSnippets(llmResult.steps || []),
